title: Timeline from start and end date columns
excerpt: How can we create a column that takes 2 date columns (start and end) and converting them to a timeline field? Didnt think this was necessary but the gantt widgets and calendar only work with timelines otherwise they are …

posts.author: lebmelvin (leb melvin)
posts.cookedHtml: How can we create a column that takes 2 date columns (start and end) and converting them to a timeline field? Didnt think this was necessary but the gantt widgets and calendar only work with timelines otherwise they are unable to display properly.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @lebmelvin

There is an app for that in the marketplace called “Start + End = Timeline”

posts.author: dralph (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml:
@basdebruin
Will it actually be a timeline column in Monday? If not a Gantt Chart will not population correctly.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @dralph

Yes, the app read two dates columns and syncs it to a real monday.com Timeline column.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @basdebruin - And your app will also work with the new Timeline & Numbers combo (timeline and duration combo columns)? Or no?
~ Deb

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @PolishedGeek , Deb

Yes, it does work with the new combo (timeline connected to numbers). In fact, since today you can configure bidirectional synching (Dates>Timeline and Timeline>Dates) as the app recognizes infinite loops and breaks the loop after the first sync is done.

With the combo: a timeline change because of the duration change will updates the start and end dates. Changing the dates will update the timeline AND the duration (numbers column).

posts.author: Caleb_Christensen (Caleb Christensen)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to be able to set the timeline for a task and have the end date of the timeline automatically populated the due date for that task. Is this possible?

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Caleb_Christensen! At the moment, there’s not a way to set an end date on the timeline but we are looking to add this capability. I’d love to understand your use case in more depth so we can understand how to best implement this in the future! Cheers!

posts.author: iain (Iain...)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also be interested in this feature, we are managing using the timeline mostly but it would be ideal for the due date to automatically populate based on the end of the timeline period - which i turn is moved out but the dependencies.

posts.author: water_bear (Todd Krupa)
posts.cookedHtml: We love how timeline columns can be set to deadline mode and sync up with status – great way to plan out workloads over the week. I was bummed to see that the “due date” automations are unavailable to this method of working. Shouldn’t it be that due “due date” = End day of Timeline, for those that are set to deadline mode?

posts.author: mkingsland (Morgan Kingsland)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a similar need! We use the timeline more as a reference and sync our final due date to status. I would love for the timeline to automatically calculate with the date the item was created as the start and final due date as end. This way, if the final due date changes, the timeline will change with it.

Ex: Today I input a request for a flyer due March 5th. The timeline would populate from Feb 25-March 5. I then change the due date to March 20, and the timeline automatically updates to reflect that final due date without changing the start date.

posts.author: jnelson_mserp (Joshua Nelson)
posts.cookedHtml: I have a similar need without automation that can be associated with the timeline, the Due Date has to be used. Without some sort of logic you find yourself adding these in 1 by 1.

posts.author: maleroy (Matthieu Leroy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday fans,

I have imported a long list of budget breakdown (1588 pulses) with a column “Start date” and a column “End date”. I would like to use these two columns as inputs for a new timeline column. Any idea on how to do that?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @maleroy , Matthieu,

I suggest to use Integromat for this. It is a bit of a learning curve, but I am confident this can be done by iterating through all the items of the board / group and then use the two dates to create a timeline out of it.

posts.author: nicolaspratt (Nicolas Pratt)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that automation of the timeline, or using it as a reference point for dashboards/views would be great. Currently it’s quite annoying to have to select the start/end date of a timeline to add a few days to the end.
Ideally there would be an automation that could pull data from a Date field and auto-populate/update the timeline column with this information.

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Thanks so much for all of this great input.

While the automations aren’t able to automatically input dates within the timeline at this time, there are a few functions I do want to mention that may be helpful in a few of the scenarios you’ve described:

  • Any automation including “set date” can be set up to automatically input dates into your Date columns. You can find these by searching “set date” in the automation center!
  • We have a few dependency automations that would enable you to adjust existing dates in a timeline or a date column based on changes made in another timeline or date column of the same item. You can find these under the Dependency category in the automation center.

I know these functions will not fit for some cases, and I definitely see the value in being able to automatically set dates within the Timeline column. This is a request that our team is aware of, and we’ll be sure to share updates as we have more information!

posts.author: cgrynberg (Caio Grynberg)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same request. I’ve imported a list from Excel originally generated from MS Planner with 2 columns one for start and one for end date. As I understand, I need to convert these to timelines to be able to see it in a Gantt Chart. I won’t do it manually - 1,000+ lines across 10+ boards, and it seems silly to have to use a 3rd party to perform this basic task in a PM software. Any other way of doing this?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @cgrynberg, @maleroy

I have an app with a feature that uses this configuration sentence:

image1238×128 11.6 KB

It is still in development (expect to release it in a few days).

Can you let me know what column types you are using to hold the start and end-dates? Are these monday Date columns or text columns holding a date in a specific format. The feature is currently using monday Data columns but i case you use a specific format please let me know how that format looks like. I can try to incorporate other date formats in this app.

As the conversion is triggered by a status changing to a value (e.g. “Convert”) it can easily be used to change large number of rows in one go as you can multi select the items where you want the conversion to take place.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @cgrynberg, @maleroy

The automation feature is now adde to the app, see Monday app with automations released - #3 by basdebruin
Hope it helps you migrating and converting, have fun!

posts.author: Elliot_BO (Elliot Thiebaut)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello !

Does anyone have any news or trick to automate the population of start and end date of a timeline ?

In my case I would love to set the start date of the timeline when the item is created and update the end date as the deadline changes.
So then I can see when I strated working on a task and when I finaly complketed it without changing everything manually…

Thanks in advance for any replies !

posts.author: anayer (Anand)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bas,

I am currently testing your app. Is there a way to use a formula column as an input rather than a date column?
Use case: We calculate an actual start and end date for a task because quite often our tasks are delayed and therefore we would like to generate a new timeline based on these inputs.

Thanks

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @anayer

I don’t think this will be possible as the output of a formula column can never be used in any automation. The output is generated client side (within you browser) and the backend (where the automations live) is not aware of these “client side columns”.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)

Create a board template (pretty simple task list, always at the same time intervals based on the “start date”) so that whenever I create the board that is time 0 if you will, and then in my template all of the “due dates” in the respective date columns auto populate based on a set delay/leadtime from the creation date.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @gallagm2 & @robertrizzo - You can do this! You have to use the app functionality and create what’s called a Workspace Template. This creates a set of one or more templates that will automatically adjust the dates: (31) monday - Apps — Mozill...

If you would like some help migrating your existing templates to be Workspace Templates, you can book a time with me here and I’ll be happy to walk you through it: Book a call with Polished Geek: Do more with monday․com

posts.author: arthur.bershadskiy (Arthur Bershadskiy)
posts.cookedHtml:
@monday-team Please add this ability. Either being able to populate the timeline based on start/end dates or vice versa would be a huge help. You can then drive the dates of the sub items using automate and dependencies.

Subitems of New Board1147×296 39.5 KB

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
We sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here: Automatically populate due date with end day in timeline - #15 - as we plan to close this smaller thread in an attempt to consolidate all votes, and better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

Thanks again!

posts.author: Christophe (SC)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

When I prepare a task, I need to enter a timeline and a time estimate. Sadly, both are not linked together.
I would prefer to enter either the start date or the end date, the time estimate to realize the task and get the timeline calculated automatically based on the start/end date and the time estimate.

With such an automated timeline definition, we could also integrate much easier the management of employees vacations: the timeline could integrate the vacation period and postpone the end date or start date depending on the initial criteria.

This suggestion can be linked to the following about User presence management: /#/t/15720?u=christophe

Thanks for any input and I hope this will catch your attention.

posts.author: papaconn (Nicholas Papaconstantinou)

posts.author: JustAnotherPM (Stuart Hooker)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is a rather (read very) clunky way to update the timeline if you need this for a one-off upload. Assuming you have a board with line-items already populated (potentially including separate start and end date columns) and an Item ID column , you can download the board to Excel. Using formulas you can then create a standard Mutate API command (see example below) insert the dates for the timeline, copy-paste the values into the API Playground, hit prettify and then execute.

mutation {
Update1: change_multiple_column_values(
item_id: xxxxxxxxxx
board_id: xxxxxxxxxx
column_values: “{"project_timeline" : {"from" : "2023-03-28", "to" : "2023-05-30"}}”
) {
id
}
Update2: change_multiple_column_values(
item_id: xxxxxxxxxx
board_id: xxxxxxxxxx
column_values: “{"project_timeline" : {"from" : "2023-09-15", "to" : "2023-12-12"}}”
) {
id
}
}

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone,

Thanks for sharing your feedback and ideas! After reading your feedback, the product team is looking into different ways to bring this feature to life. As of now, adding the ability to create a timeline from two date columns is on our long term roadmap.

Keep sharing and voting your favorite features! Your feedback truly helps our product team make decisions about our future roadmap.

All you need to know about our new Feature Request process Feature requests
Welcome to the feature request part of our community! This is your opportunity to get involved in our development process. Share and vote on features you would like to see implemented in the platform so we can build a roadmap together that best reflects your needs. How do you submit a feature request? First, check if your idea already exists in the "feature request" category and vote on that topic. That way, our team can see which features are most requested. You can use the search function o…

posts.author: JimenaB (Jimena Batschelet )
posts.cookedHtml: It’s frustrating that we need to use a separate app just to automate something as simple as setting the start and end dates of a timeline. Why isn’t this feature available directly?

posts.author: ColinCampbell (Colin Campbell)
posts.cookedHtml: What is considered long-term? I am surprised there isn’t a workaround like even importing from excel.

posts.author: chris.hiebert (Chris Hiebert)
posts.cookedHtml: Long Term? Well, it’s been 2 years since the last message indicating this feature was on the “long term roadmap”. My guess is another two years…

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community!
Amber here from the product team. I’m excited to share that we have added this request to our roadmap and are hoping to release this feature later this year. All thanks to your feedback!
Looking forward to getting this feature out to you as soon as possible while making sure we maintain the best user experience

posts.author: serenaw (Serena Wong)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com product team, my team has started using Monday.com as part of evaluating this platform for my organization. The need to populate the start and end date columns into timeline is very important because we want to use certain widgets like workload but this widget can only take in timeline field, rather than start and end dates. We imported our current project work breakdown structure task dates into Monday via excel. Unfortunately import excel can only accept date fields (another limitation on monday.com). My team can’t possibly manually update the timeline fields for more than 1000 tasks in a single project board and thus unable to monitor the resource workload using workload widget.

Together with other community members here, we are surprised this useful standard feature is not implemented yet after 2 years and should not be done via a 3rd party application. Appreciate if you can expedite this to be rolled out asap. Thank you.

posts.author: vlevasseur (Vincent Levasseur)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks a lot, it will simplify a lot of workarounds

posts.author: dorth (Dana)
posts.cookedHtml:
This was added to roadmap in March to be released this year.
Is there a date when this will be released?

posts.author: Leigh_73 (Leigh Skilton)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news on a rollout date please?

posts.author: addilovell (Addi)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love a free basic automation exactly like this. It would make many things so much simpler and easy to use.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone! Thank you for your patience around this feature. I just spoke with @amber and the boards team to get a better understanding of the expected timeline and development of this feature. Due to other priorities and our teams capacity, we do not have a confirmed timeline for the release of this feature. Our boards team is currently focusing on finishing the development of other highly requested board features, such as the ability to reorder views, wrap text, and undo capabilities

While the ability to create a timeline from start and end date columns does not currently have a definitive timeline, we are working on revamping the timeline column based on your feedback. As soon as I have more information about this, I will let you know!

posts.author: Benkazinik (Ben Kazinik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Want to learn more about how to create a proper project timeline? Here’s a great post we did on the best way to create a project timeline

monday-project-timeline1600×834 161 KB

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad to see this is on the long-term roadmap, but hope it can be moved up. This is something for which we are doing a lot of work arounds. Due to reporting limitations for the start and end dates in a timeline column, we are doing the reverse, using apps to set the start and end date from the timeline, in order to report on it in some of the widgets. If reporting could be smoothed out so the start and end dates of the timeline could be used more robustly that would help tremendously.

posts.author: JasHam (Jasmine Hammond)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there - My name is Jasmine and I’m the Community Programs Manager at monday.com. There is a current work around to this ask that our CX team has made a video to show. I hope this is helpful in the meantime while this feature release is being assessed by product!
Using monday Workflows to populate timeline start/end | Loom

I hope his is helpful.

posts.author: koko03_30 (Konstantin Botev)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, everyone,

If you are looking for a way to automate the timeline, based on different triggers, you can try the Timeline manipulator app:

auth.monday.com

Timeline manipulator - monday.com Marketplace

Automations trigger timeline updates when column values change.

Best regards,
Konstantin

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @lebmelvin - you might find our brand-new Smart Timeline + Duration app really helpful. It extends the native Timeline and Duration columns in ways that aren’t possible out of the box, all powered through automation, making your workflows way more dynamic and efficient.

Let me know if you’d like a live walkthrough or to try it out, happy to help!

posts.cookedHtml:
Hey this is possible with Autoboost! Attached is a short GIF showing me doing it just now.
Here is more info about Autoboost
it also allows many more automations including sub-item, mirror and formula columns and much more! Hope this helps

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @JasHam Thank you for this information! Not having the monday Workflows available when we setup a number of our standard boards, we went the opposite direction on this using the General Caster app. We have the user set the Timeline and GC populates the date columns. The date columns being necessary for full reporting in the widgets, as you can’t do everything with timeline for reporting that you can with dates. I’m on the constant lookout for when we can simplify this in our boards and use just dates or just timeline. In the meantime, now that Automations have come to our account, I will explore this option you shared. Thank you for sharing!

posts.author: viktoriaj (Viktoria Jacyno)
posts.cookedHtml:
Right, but the ap doesn’t meet whatever threshold my company’s security team has for using it.

Any success in getting some guarantees about privacy and security?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @viktoriaj

Are there any specific security and privacy concerns? Our EULA has a section about Customer Data.

The app does not store any data from your board. The communication between the monday infrastructure and ours is secured by encryption and monday provides tokens that are just valid for 30 seconds.

posts.author: locateparcels (Locate Parcels)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a thoughtful suggestion — having separate start and end times in a date column would really help with precise scheduling and clarity for workflows. When data is clear and visible, it becomes much easier for teams to track progress and avoid confusion. That same principle applies in logistics too: knowing exactly when something starts and ends — like a delivery window — makes planning smoother overall. Thanks for starting this useful conversation!
